one_time_description: Flooding claim settlement recognized as revenue in 2024 ($78,556,249); prior year had Timber claim settlement of $21,112,491
economic_notes: Economic development entities include Ojibways of Onigaming FN Economic Development Inc., Economic Development LP, and Crow Lake Gas & Convenience Ltd. Two entities (Kakagi Lake Enterprises LP and 1833389 Ontario Inc.) accounted for under modified equity method. Casino Rama revenue through OFNLP.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to: (1) no budget comparison presented as required by PSAS, and (2) ARO liabilities not identified per PS 3280. Auditor firm name not legible in text (signed Winnipeg, Manitoba, Chartered Professional Accountants). Massive flooding claim settlement of $78.6M dominates 2024 revenue; prior year had $21.1M timber claim settlement — both are one-time items. Accumulated surplus on balance sheet is $30,337,391 but accumulated operating surplus per surplus statement is $30,529,234 — difference of $191,843 equals accumulated remeasurement losses. Two entities (Kakagi Lake Enterprises LP and 1833389 Ontario Inc.) accounted for by modified equity method and not fully consolidated. expenses_housing combines Band Housing ($611,088) and CMHC ($510,630). expenses_enterprises combines Economic Development ($233,739), Crow Lake Gas ($1,634,443) and Economic Development entities ($17,703 + $2,000 + plus OFNLP Casino Rama $538,529 partially). total_government_transfers includes ISC $7,173,277 + Health Canada $6,699,668 + Province of Ontario $2,470,084 + CMHC $501,921 = $16,844,950. osr calculated as total revenue minus government transfers.
